# Hello Rainbow UI — Requirements Subtask

This repository holds the one-page requirements document for a UI that displays a "Hello World" message with rainbow styling and animations, plus automated checks that verify the requirements document contains the expected sections and key terms.

Run steps:

1. Install dependencies:

   npm install

2. Run tests (validates the requirements document):

   npm test

What this subtask provides:

- docs/requirements.md — the one-page functional & visual requirements (the primary artifact for this subtask).
- tests/requirements.test.ts — automated tests that assert the requirements file includes required items.

Notes:
- This is a documentation subtask; no UI implementation is included yet.

# Hello World - Rainbow UI Requirements

Version: 0.1.0
Last updated: 2025-10-11
Author: UI Team — Requirements Subtask

Overview

This document describes the exact functional and visual requirements for a single-page UI that displays a "Hello World" message with rainbow styling and subtle animations. It defines what the user should see and how the interface should behave across target platforms, plus accessibility expectations and acceptance criteria.

1. Primary text content

- Visible text: "Hello World"
- Exact capitalization and spacing: capital "H" and "W", one space between words: Hello World
- A short accessible label for screen readers should be present and match the visible text unless an enhanced description is provided.

2. Target platforms and environments

- Primary target: modern web browsers (desktop and mobile) built with standard HTML/CSS/JS.
- Supported viewport widths: responsive from 320px (small phones) to 1920px (large desktops).
- The UI must be usable on both touch and pointer devices.
- No reliance on browser-specific proprietary APIs.

3. Visual design

- Layout: a single centered message on the page. Both vertical and horizontal centering within the viewport.
- Typography: use a legible sans-serif system stack (e.g., system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, "Helvetica Neue", Arial).
- Font size examples (fluid/responsive): base font-size 32px on desktop, scale down appropriately on small screens (minimum readable size: 18px).

4. Color and rainbow styling

- The message text must display a visually continuous rainbow across the characters or as an animated gradient applied to the text fill.
- Use the following representative rainbow stops (hex):
  - Red: #FF0000
  - Orange: #FF7F00
  - Yellow: #FFFF00
  - Green: #00FF00
  - Blue: #0000FF
  - Indigo: #4B0082
  - Violet: #8B00FF

- Visual intent: the rainbow should be vivid but maintain accessible contrast against the background.
- Background: neutral solid color, default white (#FFFFFF) or very light gray to maintain contrast.

5. Animations

- Entrance: Fade-in on page load for the whole message (duration: nominal 400ms to 600ms).
- Rainbow animation: subtle continuous movement of the rainbow gradient across the text (e.g., slow horizontal gradient shift or hue rotation). Movement should be gentle (one full cycle no faster than 8s and no faster than once every 4s for smaller animations) and loop infinitely.
- Motion alternatives: Respect the user's operating system/agent preference for reduced motion (prefers-reduced-motion). When prefers-reduced-motion is set, all non-essential motion must be disabled — provide a static rainbow or solid-color fallback with no animation.

6. Interaction and states

- No complex interactive controls are required for this page; the message is informational.
- Keyboard focus: Ensure any focusable elements on the page (if present, e.g., a skip link) follow visible focus outlines that meet accessibility contrast.

7. Accessibility

- Semantic HTML: the greeting should be in a heading element (e.g., <h1>) for significance and screen reader discovery.
- ARIA: avoid unnecessary ARIA; if an alternate description exists, it should be exposed via aria-describedby rather than overriding the text.
- Contrast: ensure text (or its visual effect) meets at least AA contrast against the background. If gradient text causes contrast evaluation difficulties, provide a solid high-contrast fallback (e.g., black #000000) when needed.
- Reduced motion: honor prefers-reduced-motion and provide clear, non-animated equivalents.
- Screen reader: the message must be readable by assistive tech; do not rely solely on images to convey the text.

8. Performance and responsiveness

- The UI should load quickly; animations must be implemented using CSS transforms or opacity where possible to avoid layout thrashing.
- No large assets required.

9. Implementation notes (non-normative)

- Implement gradient text using background-clip: text and animated background-position or CSS variables/hue-rotate filters.
- Use prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable animations.

10. Success criteria (acceptance tests)

A. Visual correctness
   - The page displays the text "Hello World" centered in the viewport.
   - The text shows rainbow styling using the provided color stops or a visually equivalent rainbow gradient.
   - Entrance fade and gentle rainbow animation are present by default.

B. Accessibility
   - The text is in a semantic heading element (<h1>) and is discoverable by screen readers.
   - When the system or user agent reports prefers-reduced-motion, no non-essential animations run.
   - Text has an accessible fallback color that meets contrast requirements when the gradient alone does not.

C. Platform & responsiveness
   - The layout scales and remains legible between 320px and 1920px viewports.

D. Testability
   - An automated test verifies the requirements document contains the following explicit items: the exact visible text "Hello World", the words "prefers-reduced-motion", at least one of the rainbow hex colors listed above (e.g., #FF0000), and a success criteria section describing acceptance tests.

11. Deliverables for implementation task

- One-page UI (HTML/CSS/JS) implementing the above visual behavior.
- A11y checks and a short demo page for manual review.

End of requirements.

notes: Run `npm install` then `npm test` to validate the requirements document.
This subtask provides a single requirements page (docs/requirements.md) plus tests that assert the document contains required sections and keywords.
